text: ‘Heaping up a store of retribution’; in the Greek, ‘heaping up treasure’.
‘The innocent man’; this might also be translated ‘the Just One’, and understood as a reference to our Lord; cf. Ac. 3.14; 7.52; Wis. 2.12-20.
In Palestine, the rains of early autumn and late spring are especially important to the crops.
‘How kind and merciful the Lord is in rewarding us’; literally, ‘the fulfilment of the Lord, how kind and merciful he is’. Cf. Rom. 6.21; I Pet. 1.9.

Cf. Mt. 5.33. It seems probable that St James had some special reason for warning the Jewish Christians against taking oaths, perhaps because he was afraid of their taking part in unlawful conspiracies.
‘Give him relief’; in the Greek, ‘raise him up’.
That is, probably, his own sins; cf. I Pet. 4.8.
